title: lippia ciliata
intro: Lippia ciliata, also known as Mexican oregano, is a shrubby plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It possesses a rich history of traditional use in folk medicine, valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, Lippia ciliata is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for a range of applications from culinary use to therapeutic treatment.
